scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 2024

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

3. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date fixed for performance or when the plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell whereby the Defendant agreed to sell and the Plaintiff agreed to purchase a residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement included the execution of the sale deed on or before 10.07.2024 against payment of the balance consideration. The Agreement to Sell is marked as Annexure A.

5. On the date of the agreement, i.e., 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. The Defendant acknowledged receipt of the said amount. The bank receipt evidencing the payment is marked as Annexure B.

6. The balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid on or before 10.07.2024 at the time of execution of the sale deed. The Plaintiff has always remained ready and willing to perform her part of the contract and has arranged the necessary funds to pay the balance consideration within the stipulated timeline.

7. The Agreement to Sell was executed at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}} and is purported to be registered under {{REGISTRATION_DETAILS}}. The property description is more fully detailed in the Schedule of Property attached hereto. The Plaintiff has not been put in possession of the suit property as the possession was agreed to be handed over upon execution of the sale deed and payment of the full consideration.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

8. On 05.06.2024, prior to the date fixed for execution of the sale deed, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff expressly refusing to execute the sale deed. In the said letter, the Defendant claimed that he had received a higher offer for the suit property from a third party and therefore would not honor the agreement entered into with the Plaintiff. The Defendant's refusal letter is marked as Annexure D.

9. On 20.06.2024, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed in terms of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 and to receive the balance consideration. The notice was sent via post and acknowledged due. The legal notice along with the postal receipt is marked as Annexure C.

10. Despite receipt of the legal notice, the Defendant has failed and refused to execute the sale deed or to complete the transaction in accordance with the agreed terms. The Defendant has not communicated any willingness to perform his obligations under the agreement and has maintained his refusal to sell the suit property to the Plaintiff at the agreed price.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

11. Due to the Defendant's refusal to execute the sale deed, the Plaintiff has been deprived of the opportunity to acquire the suit property at the agreed consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The Plaintiff has suffered loss as the market value of similar properties in the vicinity has risen since the date of the agreement, resulting in financial prejudice to the Plaintiff.

12. The advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- paid by the Plaintiff remains blocked due to the Defendant's breach, preventing the Plaintiff from utilizing said funds for other investment or purchase opportunities. The Plaintiff has incurred costs towards the preparation of the agreement and the issuance of the legal notice to enforce her rights.

13. The Plaintiff has taken steps to mitigate the loss by issuing the legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to perform his obligations. However, the Defendant's continued refusal has left the Plaintiff with no option but to approach this Court for relief.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

15. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

16. The Plaintiff is also entitled to compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant communicated his refusal to execute the sale deed, thereby breaching the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024. The cause of action continues to subsist as the Defendant has not performed his obligations.

18. The breach occurred when the Defendant refused to execute the sale deed despite the Plaintiff's readiness and willingness to perform her part of the contract.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

19. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in respect of the suit property in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration of Rs. {{BALANCE_CONSIDERATION}}/-;
    (b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff;
    (c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
    (d) Award pendente lite and future interest on the amount paid under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 2024.

I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 19 of this plaint are true and correct
